examples.title: Calculate Average Waiting Time
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate waiting time for FCFS scheduling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_Waiting_Time_FCFS
INPUT: Array of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Average waiting time
BEGIN
  n ← number of processes
  waiting_time[0] ← 0
  total_wait ← 0
  
  FOR i ← 1 TO n-1 DO
    current_burst ← burst_time[i-1]
    waiting_time[i] ← current_burst + waiting_time[i-1]
    total_wait ← total_wait + waiting_time[i]
  END FOR
  
  avg_waiting ← total_wait / n
  RETURN avg_waiting
END
examples.explanation: For FCFS, waiting time of each process is the sum of burst times of all previous processes. This algorithm accumulates the total waiting time.

examples.title: Calculate Turnaround Time
examples.description: Python function to compute turnaround time for all processes
examples.code:
def calculate_turnaround_time(burst_time, waiting_time):
    n = len(burst_time)
    turnaround_time = []
    
    for i in range(n):
        tat = waiting_time[i] + burst_time[i]
        turnaround_time.append(tat)
    
    return turnaround_time

# Example usage
burst_time = [10, 5, 8]
waiting_time = [13, 0, 5]

tat = calculate_turnaround_time(burst_time, waiting_time)
print("Turnaround times:", tat)
print("Average TAT:", sum(tat)/len(tat))
examples.explanation: Turnaround Time = Waiting Time + Burst Time. This simple formula shows that minimizing waiting time directly minimizes turnaround time.

examples.title: SJF Scheduling - Find Minimum Burst
examples.description: Selecting next process with shortest burst time
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SJF_Schedule
INPUT: List of processes in ready queue with their remaining burst times
OUTPUT: Execution order
BEGIN
  ready_queue ← list of processes with burst times
  completed ← 0
  current_time ← 0
  
  WHILE completed < total_processes DO
    shortest_job ← NULL
    min_burst ← INFINITY
    
    FOR each process p IN ready_queue DO
      IF p.arrival_time <= current_time AND p.remaining_burst < min_burst THEN
        shortest_job ← p
        min_burst ← p.remaining_burst
      END IF
    END FOR
    
    IF shortest_job != NULL THEN
      Execute shortest_job for its burst time
      current_time ← current_time + shortest_job.burst
      Remove shortest_job from ready_queue
      completed ← completed + 1
    ELSE
      current_time ← current_time + 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: SJF always selects the process with the smallest burst time from the ready queue. This minimizes average waiting time but may cause starvation for long processes.

examples.title: Round Robin Time Slice Execution
examples.description: Simulating Round Robin scheduling with time quantum
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Round_Robin_Schedule
INPUT: Processes with arrival times and burst times, time_quantum
OUTPUT: Execution sequence and waiting times
BEGIN
  ready_queue ← queue of processes sorted by arrival time
  current_time ← 0
  time_quantum ← given time slice
  
  WHILE ready_queue is not empty DO
    current_process ← ready_queue.pop_front()
    
    IF current_process.remaining_burst > time_quantum THEN
      Execute for time_quantum
      current_time ← current_time + time_quantum
      current_process.remaining_burst ← current_process.remaining_burst - time_quantum
      ready_queue.push_back(current_process)
    ELSE
      Execute remaining burst
      current_time ← current_time + current_process.remaining_burst
      current_process.remaining_burst ← 0
      Calculate turnaround and waiting times
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Round Robin gives each process equal time slices. It provides good response time for interactive systems and ensures no starvation since all processes get CPU time cyclically.
